name: Pineapple Upside-Down Cupcakes
recipeIngredient: 1 large egg, separated, at room temperature
1/2 cup reserved pineapple juice (from above can)
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly grease each 2 1/2 inch cup in a 12-cup muffin tin.
2. For topping, melt butter in a small saucepan. Stir in sugar and cinnamon until blended. Remove from heat and add half cup of the pineapple (save remainder for another use). Spoon about 1 tablespoon of topping into each muffin cup.
3. Place a cherry half, round side down, in center of each.
4. In a small bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
5. In another bowl, beat egg white with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form.
6. In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar with an electric mixer (no need to clean beaters) until fluffy. Beat in egg yolk and vanilla extract.
7. Alternately add flour mixture and pineapple juice, beating well after each addition.
8. Gently fold beaten egg white into batter.
9. Pour evenly into muffin cups.
10. Bake at 350°F for 20 to 25 minutes or until cupcakes test done.
11. Cool in pan on wire rack for 10 minutes. Invert onto cookie sheet and gently tap each cup to release cupcakes.
12. Serve warm or at room temperature.
